chapter_title.en: Chapter 10 – The intention in the swearing of the oath

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/322)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/322)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ حَلَفَ وَ ضَمِيرُهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ مَا حَلَفَ قَالَ الْيَمِينُ عَلَى الضَّمِيرِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ismail Bin Saeed Al Ashary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who swore and oath and his conscience was upon other than what he had sworn. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The oath is upon the conscience’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ’Isma‘il ibn Sa‘d al-Ash‘ariy who has narrated the following: “I once asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who swears but his mind is on something other than what he has taken oath for. He (the Imam) said, ‘The oath is for that which is in one’s mind.’”
